method_atom: a gauge theory in which the connection is treated as an independent dynamical variable and a spontaneous symmetry-breaking mechanism is driven by auxiliary order-parameter fields that acquire a nonzero configuration, reducing the original gauge symmetry to a smaller residual symmetry. The symmetry breaking dynamically selects a nondegenerate structure and generates emergent geometric/relational structures, providing a Higgs-like route to unifying separate interaction sectors into one framework.
object_atom: the gravitational field on spacetime, described through an independent connection together with a soldering form and a fiber metric acting as order parameters, with the aim of explaining the nondegeneracy of the spacetime metric and unifying gravity with the other fundamental interactions.
brief_reason: The transferable part is the order-parameter-driven gauge-symmetry-breaking (Higgs) mechanism acting on an independent connection; the domain-specific part is its application to spacetime geometry (metric, soldering form) and the unification of gravity.
